buttons.overrideAll: Replace all files in destination folder

upload.abortUpload: Are you sure you wish to abort?

cnc.statusTooltip: Streaming to machine: {path} — click to open the file
cnc.recoveryBanner: Previous job ({path}) didn't end cleanly. Verify the part + machine state before starting a new one.

machine.dashboardNotConfigured: Set the dashboard URL in Settings → Machine to embed it here.
machine.notConfigured: Set the Haas host in Settings → Machine
machine.queueEmpty: No files queued. Open an NC file and click 'Send to machine' to stage it here.
machine.queueSendTitle: Send this file (pick MEM or DNC)
machine.queueAutoSendTitle: Auto-send: preflight + Start in one click (MEM). CYCLE START is still on you.
machine.queueBusy: RS-232 busy — wait for the active send to finish
machine.viewerIdle: Toolpath renders here once a job is running.
machine.ncIdle: No NC file in flight. Send one from the file browser to see G-code here.
machine.filePreviewUnsupported: Preview not supported — open the file from the file browser to download.
machine.activityEmpty: No events yet — start a job to see streamer activity here.
machine.rtspNotSupported: Browsers cannot play raw RTSP/RTSPS. For UniFi Protect or Reolink, switch the camera type to Iframe in Settings → Machine and paste the web Live View URL. For HLS, run a go2rtc / MediaMTX proxy and paste the .m3u8.

toolTable.title: Tool table
toolTable.readNowTitle: Reads tool length / wear / diameter for every populated slot. Two-pass: cheap length-first scan across all slots, then full read on the populated ones. Refuses during a job.
toolTable.empty: No tool-table read on file yet. Run one to populate.
toolTable.slotCountTitle: How many tool slots to read (1-200). Empty pockets are skipped after a quick length probe; populated slots get the full 4-base read.
toolTable.persistFailed: Read OK but history dump failed
toolTable.noMatch: No slots match the current filter.

sendWizard.title: Send to machine
sendWizard.methodMemBody: Program uploads into the controller's memory, then runs from the local copy. RS-232 link can disconnect after upload with no impact. Best for any file that fits in NC memory.
sendWizard.methodDncBody: Bytes execute on the controller as they arrive. Required only for programs too big to fit in NC memory. Mid-stream RS-232 hiccup will alarm and abort the cut.
sendWizard.memStep2: Cursor onto the empty slot at the end of memory
sendWizard.memStep3: Press [RECEIVE] (or RECV softkey). Then click Send below.
sendWizard.dncStep3: Press [INSERT] / [SELECT PROG]. Then click Send below; press [CYCLE START] once the program is loaded.
sendWizard.prereqHint: Run a connection check before sending — Send is locked until both bridge and controller respond.
sendWizard.requirePreflightNote: This machine is configured to require a preflight pass — Send will be refused if any program tool isn't loaded in the latest tool-table dump.
sendWizard.toolCheckLoading: Parsing NC and joining against latest tool table…
sendWizard.toolCheckNoTable: No tool-table read on file for this machine — flying blind. Run a tool-table read on /machine first to get diameter / wear comparisons.
sendWizard.toolCheckNoTools: No T-codes found in the program — nothing to verify.
sendWizard.spindleSwap: spindle currently has T{from} → program will swap to T{to}
sendWizard.spindleMatch: spindle has T{n} (no swap on first M06)
sendWizard.cutterCompTitle: Program activates G41/G42 cutter compensation while this tool is selected — diameter offset must be non-zero in the tool table

errors.forbidden: You don't have permissions to access this.
errors.cncJobAlreadyRunning: A job is already streaming to the machine.
errors.cncSettingsMissing: Set the Haas host in Settings → Machine before sending.
errors.cncRecoveryPending: Previous job didn't end cleanly. Acknowledge the recovery banner before starting a new job.

files.name: Name
files.noPreview: Preview is not available for this file.
files.csvTooLarge: CSV file is too large for preview (>5MB). Please download to view.

ctrl.s: save a file or download the directory where you are

help.esc: clear selection and/or close the prompt

login.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least {min} characters

logout_reasons.inactivity: You have been logged out due to inactivity.

prompts.copyMessage: Choose the location to copy your files to:
prompts.deleteMessageMultiple: Are you sure you wish to delete {count} file(s)?
prompts.deleteMessageSingle: Are you sure you wish to delete this file/folder?
prompts.deleteMessageShare: Are you sure you wish to delete this share({path})?
prompts.deleteUser: Are you sure you want to delete this user?
prompts.downloadMessage: Choose the format you wish to download.
prompts.moveMessage: Choose new home for your file(s)/folder(s):
prompts.newArchetype: Create a new post based on an archetype. Your file will be created on content folder.
prompts.replaceMessage: One of the files you're trying to upload has a conflicting name. Do you wish to skip this file and continue to upload or replace the existing one?
prompts.scheduleMessage: Pick a date and time to schedule the publication of this post.
prompts.discardEditorChanges: Are you sure you wish to discard the changes you've made?
prompts.stopMachineHint: The Pi will stop feeding bytes. The Haas will drain its look-ahead buffer and halt within seconds.
prompts.resolveConflict: Which files do you want to keep?
prompts.singleConflictResolve: If you select both versions, a number will be added to the name of the copied file.
prompts.fastConflictResolve: The destination folder there are {count} files with same name.
prompts.currentPasswordMessage: Enter your password to validate this action.

settings.allowEdit: Edit, rename and delete files or directories
settings.hideLoginButton: Hide the login button from public pages
settings.brandingHelp:
You can customize how your File Browser instance looks and feels by changing its name, replacing the logo, adding custom styles and even disable external links to GitHub.
For more information about custom branding, please check out the {0}.
settings.commandRunnerHelp: Here you can set commands that are executed in the named events. You must write one per line. The environment variables {0} and {1} will be available, being {0} relative to {1}. For more information about this feature and the available environment variables, please read the {2}.
settings.createUserDir: Auto create user home dir while adding new user
settings.tusUploadsHelp: File Browser supports chunked file uploads, allowing for the creation of efficient, reliable, resumable and chunked file uploads even on unreliable networks.
settings.tusUploadsChunkSize: Indicates to maximum size of a request (direct uploads will be used for smaller uploads). You may input a plain integer denoting byte size input or a string like 10MB, 1GB etc.
settings.tusUploadsRetryCount: Number of retries to perform if a chunk fails to upload.
settings.userHomeBasePath: Base path for user home directories
settings.userScopeGenerationPlaceholder: The scope will be auto generated
settings.defaultUserDescription: These are the default settings for new users.
settings.executeOnShellDescription: By default, File Browser executes the commands by calling their binaries directly. If you wish to run them on a shell instead (such as Bash or PowerShell), you can define it here with the required arguments and flags. If set, the command you execute will be appended as an argument. This applies to both user commands and event hooks.
settings.globalRules: This is a global set of allow and disallow rules. They apply to every user. You can define specific rules on each user's settings to override these ones.
settings.machineSettingsHelp: Configure each Haas controller. The first machine in the list is the default destination for jobs sent without an explicit machine.
settings.machineNoneError: Add at least one machine before saving.
settings.machineToolSlotsHelp: How many tool slots to read on a tool-table refresh. Set to your machine's actual pocket count (e.g. 20 for a 20-pocket carousel) — the read auto-skips empty slots after a length probe, so this just bounds the scan. 0 falls back to 30.
settings.machineRequirePreflightHelp: Server-side gate. When enabled, /api/cnc/start refuses programs whose tools aren't loaded in the latest tool-table dump (missing or empty pocket). Off by default — the wizard always soft-warns. Turn on once your tool table is reliably up to date.
settings.machineAxesHelp: X / Y / Z are always on. Check A / B / C if your machine has a 4th or 5th axis — the dashboard's position table grows rows accordingly.
settings.machinePositionToleranceHelp: Threshold for the Δ-CMD column on the /machine dashboard. Differences below this stay green; above it amber. Default 0.001".
settings.machineDprntCaptureHelp: When on, the streamer scavenges DPRNT[…] macro output between line writes during a job and surfaces each line as a live event in the activity log. Adds ~3ms per G-code line — enable only on machines that use DPRNT for in-cycle probing or telemetry.
settings.machineAutoSendEnabledHelp: When on, queue rows show an auto-send shortcut that runs preflight + Start in one step if every tool is loaded and no spindle swap is pending. CYCLE START is not triggered — the operator still presses the physical button. Off by default. Pair with Require preflight for a hard-block fallback.
settings.machineBrandHelp: Only Haas is wired into the streamer today. Other brands round-trip through settings so migration is easier when their protocol lands.
settings.machineCameraTypeHelp: Browsers cannot play raw RTSP/RTSPS. For UniFi Protect or Reolink, paste a Live View web URL and choose Iframe. For HLS, paste an .m3u8. Auto picks based on URL suffix.
settings.machineTokenHelp: Long-lived bearer for external services that call /api/cnc/state or /api/cnc/qcode without a filebrowser session — e.g. Home Assistant scripts, monitoring agents, custom dashboards. Send it in the Authorization header as a Bearer token. Regenerating invalidates the old value.
settings.toolProbeHelp: Diagnostic. Reads tool length / wear / diameter macro variables across 30 slots to confirm which Haas mapping the firmware uses. Run once when first wiring a controller, or after a firmware change. Takes ~18 seconds; refuses during a job. See docs/TOOL_TABLE_RESEARCH.md.
settings.toolLifeProbeTitle: Scan macros 3000..3199 to discover which carry tool-life data on this machine. See docs/TOOL_LIFE_RESEARCH.md.
settings.toolLifeProbeMeta: Range {range}: {ok} non-zero, {empty} zero, {err} errors in {ms} ms
settings.lockPassword: Prevent the user from changing the password

perm.rename: Rename or move files and directories

settings.permissionsHelp: You can set the user to be an administrator or choose the permissions individually. If you select "Administrator", all of the other options will be automatically checked. The management of users remains a privilege of an administrator.
settings.ruleExample1: prevents the access to any dotfile (such as .git, .gitignore) in every folder.
settings.ruleExample2: blocks the access to the file named Caddyfile on the root of the scope.
settings.rulesHelp: Here you can define a set of allow and disallow rules for this specific user. The blocked files won't show up in the listings and they wont be accessible to the user. We support regex and paths relative to the users scope.
settings.singleClick: Use single clicks to open files and directories
settings.userCommandsHelp: A space separated list with the available commands for this user. Example:
